executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Whole-stack certification remains blocked by external docs verification failures plus marketing outcome verification still failing closed.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Do not certify green until docs regains independent-pass status and marketing produces a fresh independent pass backed by measurable Codeberg movement.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus live artifact cross-checks

findings.title: Docs quality loop is the top external blocker
findings.mechanism: Current health monitoring still shows docs verifier signoff failure plus unresolved docs review/verifier follow-up items.
findings.root_cause: Docs owner artifacts are still red because repo publication drift and verifier/review follow-up remain unresolved.
findings.recommended_fix: Let the docs owner loop clear publication drift and rerun the verifier/review pipeline to a real independent pass before any whole-stack green claim.

findings.title: Marketing remains externally red on outcome evidence
findings.mechanism: Marketing independent verification is still fail/stale and the unresolved blocker remains primary-repo adoption measurement pending.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence for Codeberg-primary adoption is still missing.
findings.recommended_fix: Let the marketing owner loop produce fresh measurable outcome evidence, then rerun marketing independent verification before calling the whole stack green.

findings.title: Live Gateway topology is clean and architecture-owned runtime checks are green
findings.mechanism: Direct live cron inspection shows 21 enabled jobs, 0 disabled jobs, 0 running jobs, and 0 live last-error jobs; the remaining blockers are outside architecture ownership.
findings.root_cause: Architecture/runtime topology is coherent while docs and marketing owner loops still carry the red state.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep live-topology verification tied to direct cron inspection on each watchdog run and avoid treating external blocker clearance as an architecture repair.

findings.title: Architecture verifier path is green on freshness and ownership gates
findings.mechanism: Independent verification, loop integrity, and runtime market-intelligence consumption remain coherent after the refresh; remaining blocker classification is externalized correctly.
findings.root_cause: Architecture-side gates are healthy; remaining risk is external.
findings.recommended_fix: Rerun independent verification after each material architecture artifact refresh.

findings.title: Persisted disabled jobs remain history only, not live runtime blockers
findings.mechanism: Disabled entries still exist in jobs.json history, but live Gateway topology currently exposes zero disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Historical scheduler records persist after live job removal/disable cleanup.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live runtime topology in every audit.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Get the docs owner loop back to a real independent pass
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Docs is the strongest currently localized external blocker and still fails runtime signoff.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the docs blocker or sharpens the exact docs repair target without polluting architecture status.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Get a fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Marketing still blocks whole-stack certification even though architecture-owned gates are coherent.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the remaining adoption blocker or preserves truthful fail-closed outcome ownership.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep architecture live-topology verification tied to openclaw cron list --json on every watchdog run
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Current live inspection is clean and should remain the source of truth instead of persisted-history drift.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Stops stale topology claims from reappearing in architecture reports.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Reran agent_architecture_independent_verify.py and agent_architecture_verifier.py so the verifier artifact no longer lagged newer runtime evidence.
Rechecked live Gateway state at 21 total jobs, 21 enabled, 0 disabled, 0 running, and 0 live last-error jobs.
Confirmed the remaining red state is still externalized to docs verifier failure plus marketing independent-verification failure, not architecture runtime drift.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh docs independent pass after repo publication drift and verifier/review follow-up are cleared.
Fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Docs and marketing both remain externally red while architecture-owned runtime checks are clean
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Architecture-owned runtime checks are coherent, but docs verification/publication drift and marketing outcome evidence still prevent a truthful whole-stack green claim.

notes: Architecture green here means the architecture-owned verifier path is coherent; it does not mean the whole stack is green.
Persisted disabled jobs remain history only; live disabled jobs are 0.
Independent live inspection in this snapshot saw 21 total jobs, 21 enabled, 0 disabled, 0 running, and 0 live last-error jobs.
This run repaired the stale verifier freshness chain by rerunning independent verification and verifier signoff at 2026-05-26T01:06:19+02:00.
The remaining blockers are external docs verification failure and marketing outcome verification failure, not architecture runtime drift.
